# zer0lint

> AI memory extraction diagnostics for mem0. Finds silent extraction failures and fixes them in one command.

## What it is

zer0lint tests whether your mem0 config is actually extracting facts from agent memory. The failure mode is silent: `add()` returns success, `search()` returns results, but extraction produces malformed JSON or drops specifics. Your agent doesn't remember — no error, no warning.

Proven result (mistral:7b, default mem0 config): 0% recall on technical facts. Same model with zer0lint's extraction prompt: 100%. One config change.

## Install

pip install zer0lint

Requires: Python 3.9+, mem0 v1.x, Ollama or cloud LLM in your mem0 config.

## CLI

zer0lint check --config ~/.cogito/config.json
  Tests current config against synthetic technical facts. Returns score + HEALTHY/ACCEPTABLE/DEGRADED/CRITICAL.

zer0lint generate --config ~/.cogito/config.json
  3-phase: baseline test -> re-test with zer0lint prompt -> apply if improved. Writes validated prompt to config. Always backs up original.

zer0lint generate --config ~/.cogito/config.json --dry-run
  Shows what would change without applying.

## Key finding

In mem0 v1.x, passing a prompt via memory.add(..., prompt=X) has no measurable effect. The extraction prompt must live in the config (custom_fact_extraction_prompt field). zer0lint writes to the correct location.

## Test results (2026-03-22)

5 technical facts, model comparison:
  qwen3.5:4b  -- default: 80%,  zer0lint: 100%  (+20pp)
  mistral:7b  -- default:  0%,  zer0lint: 100%  (+100pp)

Scale test, 10 facts, 5 domains:
  default: 7/10 (70%)
  zer0lint: 9/10 (90%)

## Score thresholds

HEALTHY    >= 80%
ACCEPTABLE   60-79%
DEGRADED     40-59%
CRITICAL   < 40%

## Ecosystem

zer0lint -> cogito-ergo -> zer0dex

zer0lint (this): ingestion health layer -- fix extraction before anything else
cogito-ergo (https://github.com/roli-lpci/cogito-ergo): two-stage retrieval (vector search + integer-pointer LLM filter). Uses zer0lint's extraction prompt by default.
zer0dex (https://github.com/roli-lpci/zer0dex): dual-layer memory (compressed index + vector store). Relies on correct extraction upstream.

Recommended pipeline:
  1. zer0lint check      -- verify extraction is working
  2. zer0lint generate   -- fix if not
  3. deploy cogito-ergo  -- retrieval on clean data

## Hermes Labs suite

lintlang       -- static linter for AI agent tool descriptions and prompts
Little Canary  -- prompt injection detection
Suy Sideguy    -- runtime policy enforcement for agents
zer0lint       -- memory extraction diagnostics (this package)

https://hermes-labs.ai
